seoTitle: Cremorne vs Crescent Head — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Cremorne and Crescent Head. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Cremorne vs Crescent Head: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cremorne and Crescent Head.
heading: Cremorne vs Crescent Head — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cremorne, New South Wales and Crescent Head, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Crescent Head has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Cremorne (49/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cremorne or Crescent Head safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cremorne has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Crescent Head scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cremorne scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Crescent Head scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cremorne or Crescent Head?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Crescent Head has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
